questions.text: The covalent bond formed between two monosaccharides is called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A glycosidic bond is a specific covalent bond that connects two monosaccharides. It is formed through a dehydration synthesis reaction, where a water molecule is eliminated. This bond is essential in forming complex carbohydrates like disaccharides and polysaccharides.

Hydrogen bonds are weak, non-covalent interactions that occur between a hydrogen atom and an electronegative atom, such as nitrogen or oxygen. They play a crucial role in the secondary structure of proteins and the properties of water, but they do not link monosaccharides together.

A peptide bond is a type of covalent bond that connects amino acids to form proteins. It involves the carboxyl group of one amino acid and the amino group of another. This bond is not involved in the formation of carbohydrate structures.

A disulfide bond is a covalent bond formed between the sulfur atoms of two cysteine residues in proteins, contributing to the tertiary and quaternary structure of proteins. It has no role in connecting monosaccharides in carbohydrates.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the glycosidic bond. This bond is a covalent linkage specifically formed between two monosaccharides, resulting in the creation of disaccharides or polysaccharides through a dehydration synthesis reaction. The other options are incorrect: hydrogen bonds are non-covalent and do not connect sugars; peptide bonds are covalent but link amino acids, not sugars; and disulfide bonds stabilize protein structures and are not involved in carbohydrate bonding.

questions.text: Fats containing unsaturated fatty acids are liquid at room temperature because;
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: All the valencies of carbon are satisfied by single bonds
options.explanation: This option refers to saturated fatty acids rather than unsaturated fatty acids. Saturated fatty acids have all of their carbon atoms connected by single bonds, which means that each carbon atom has satisfied all of its available valencies with hydrogen atoms. This results in a straight and rigid carbon chain structure. Due to the absence of double bonds, the molecule is more linear, allowing the fatty acids to pack closely together and form a solid or semi-solid state at room temperature.

options.text: They contain even number of carbon atoms
options.explanation: The even number of carbon atoms is not directly related to the liquid state of unsaturated fatty acids at room temperature. The number of carbon atoms in a fatty acid chain can vary, and it does not determine whether the fatty acid is saturated or unsaturated. Both saturated and unsaturated fatty acids can have even or odd numbers of carbon atoms in their chain.

options.text: Their structure has bends which makes them more fluid
options.explanation: This statement is the correct explanation for why fats containing unsaturated fatty acids are liquid at room temperature. Unsaturated fatty acids have one or more double bonds in their carbon chain. These double bonds introduce kinks or bends in the structure, preventing the fatty acid molecules from packing closely together. The presence of these bends or kinks makes the fatty acids more fluid, leading to a liquid state at room temperature. The more double bonds present in the fatty acid chain, the more bends or kinks there will be, resulting in a more fluid nature.

options.text: Their structure is chain-like which allows them to be more fluid
options.explanation: This statement does not accurately explain the fluidity of unsaturated fatty acids. Both saturated and unsaturated fatty acids have a chain-like structure. However, it is the presence of double bonds in unsaturated fatty acids that introduces bends or kinks in the structure, making them more fluid compared to saturated fatty acids. The straight and rigid nature of saturated fatty acid chains allows them to pack closely together, leading to a solid or semi-solid state at room temperature.

questions.explanation: This statement is the correct explanation for why fats containing unsaturated fatty acids are liquid at room temperature. questions.text: Decrease in velocity per unit time is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity per unit time. It can be positive (speeding up) or negative (slowing down). For example, when a car speeds up, its velocity increases and its acceleration is positive. When a car slows down, its velocity decreases and its acceleration is negative. Deceleration, on the other hand, is a type of acceleration that occurs when an object slows down. It is also known as negative acceleration. When an object decelerates, its velocity decreases and its acceleration is negative. So, a decrease in velocity per unit time is not acceleration because it is the opposite of acceleration. It is deceleration.
A decrease in velocity per unit time is not positive acceleration. It is the opposite of positive acceleration and is called deceleration.

Deceleration is the rate at which an object slows down. It is measured as the decrease in velocity per unit time, and is given by the formula: deceleration = (final velocity - initial velocity) / timewhere "final velocity" is the velocity of the object at the end of the time interval, "initial velocity" is the velocity of the object at the beginning of the time interval, and "time" is the duration of the interval.If the final velocity is less than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is negative, indicating that the object is slowing down. If the final velocity is greater than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is positive, indicating that the object is speeding up.

A decrease in velocity per unit time is not uniform acceleration. Uniform acceleration refers to a constant rate of change in velocity over time, either positive or negative. Deceleration, on the other hand, is not uniform acceleration because the rate at which the velocity decreases is not constant over time. The rate of deceleration may change depending on the object's initial velocity, its mass, and the forces acting on it.

questions.text: If a block is suspended as shown in the given figure, then the tension in the string T3 will be equal to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect because the tension in T3 is not equal to the weight of the block. It only balances the weight vertically.

options.explanation: When a block is suspended by multiple strings, the tension in each string can be different depending on the arrangement and forces involved. In this case, we need to analyze the forces acting on the block to determine the tension in the string T3.The weight of the block acts vertically downward due to gravity, and its magnitude is given by the product of mass and acceleration due to gravity (W = mg). Let's assume the weight of the block is W.Now, let's consider the forces acting on the block. Since the block is in equilibrium (not accelerating), the net force on the block must be zero.T1: The tension in string T1 is balancing the weight of the block vertically upward. So, T1 = W.T2: The tension in string T2 is perpendicular to the weight and does not contribute to balancing it. It only provides horizontal support. The magnitude of T2 has no direct relation to the weight of the block.T3: The tension in string T3 is responsible for balancing the weight of the block both vertically and horizontally. Since the block is in equilibrium, the vertical component of T3 must equal the weight of the block, and the horizontal component of T3 must be zero.Since the vertical component of T3 balances the weight, it will be equal to W. However, the horizontal component of T3 is zero.Therefore, the tension in string T3 is equal to the weight of the block, which is half of the weight of the block.

questions.explanation: To determine the tension in the string T3, we must analyze the equilibrium of the block. The block is suspended and not moving, which means the net forces acting on it are zero. T1 balances the weight of the block vertically. T2 provides horizontal support and does not influence the vertical balance.The tension in T3 is responsible for balancing both vertical and horizontal forces. Given the system's equilibrium, the vertical component of the tension in T3 must equate to the weight of the block. However, due to the geometric arrangement of the strings and how forces distribute, T3 effectively carries half of the weight of the block.Option A, C, and B are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the distribution of forces within this specific setup. Only Option D correctly identifies that T3 is half of the weight of the block.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about newton's laws of motion?

options.text: One of them is not related to forces
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because each of Newton's three laws of motion is fundamentally related to forces. They collectively describe how forces influence the motion of objects.

options.text: Two of them are not related to forces
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. All three of Newton's laws involve forces; none of them are unrelated to forces. Each law addresses different aspects of how forces affect motion.

options.text: All of them are related to forces
options.explanation: This is the correct option. Newton's laws of motion describe the relationship between an object's motion and the forces acting upon it. Each law examines a different interaction involving forces.

options.text: One of them is related to forces
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it suggests that only one of Newton's laws involves forces. In fact, all of Newton's laws are centered around the concept of forces and how they affect motion.

questions.explanation: Newton's laws of motion are all related to forces. The first law (Law of Inertia) describes how an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a force. The second law quantifies how the force applied to an object is proportional to its acceleration (F=ma). The third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction, highlighting the interaction of forces between two bodies. Each law explains different aspects of the influence of forces on motion, making option C correct. Options A, B, and D are incorrect as they mistakenly suggest that not all of the laws are related to forces.

options.explanation: Newton's second law provides a formula for calculating force, expressed as F = ma. It quantitatively relates the net force acting on an object to its mass and the acceleration produced.

Newton's third law is about the interaction between two bodies, stating that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. It does not provide a quantitative formula for calculating force.

Newton's first law discusses inertia, describing an object's tendency to remain at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a net force. It does not provide a direct formula for calculating force.
This option is incorrect because the quantitative measure of force is accurately described by Newton's second law of motion.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Newton's second law of motion. This law gives the quantitative measure of force through the equation F = ma, where F is the force applied, m is the mass of the object, and a is the acceleration. It establishes a direct relationship between these variables, showing that force is directly proportional to the product of mass and acceleration.Newton's third law, while significant, focuses on the principle of action and reaction between two interacting bodies and does not provide a formula for force. Newton's first law addresses the concept of inertia, outlining an object's resistance to changes in its state of motion, without offering a quantitative expression for force.

questions.text: If we are standing in a bus and the driver applies a brake then we feel:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In the context of the bus braking, you actually feel like you are being pushed forward, not backward. This sensation comes from your body's inertia resisting the change in motion as the bus decelerates. The force that you perceive as acting backwards is not a pseudo force, but rather the result of inertia in a non-inertial frame of reference.

When the bus brakes, your body continues to move forward due to inertia, while the bus slows down. This creates the sensation of being pushed forward, which is a pseudo force perceived in the non-inertial frame of the braking bus. This forward push is because your body's inertia resists the bus's deceleration.

The sensation of being pushed backward is not due to a real force. It is actually your body's inertia that causes you to continue moving forward as the bus decelerates. There is no actual force pushing you backward; instead, it's the lack of force continuing to move you forward that creates this sensation.

A real force does not push you forward when the bus brakes. The forward sensation is due to inertia, not a real force. The only real force involved is the deceleration force applied by the brakes on the bus, not directly on you.

questions.explanation: When a bus decelerates, passengers inside feel like they are being pushed forward due to inertia. This is because their bodies continue in motion at the original speed due to Newton's first law of motion, which states that an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by a net external force. In this scenario, a pseudo force acts forwards in the non-inertial reference frame of the bus. This is why Option B is correct. Option A is incorrect because the sensation is not backward. Option C is incorrect because the sensation of being pushed backward is not caused by a real force. Option D is incorrect because while you do feel a forward motion, it is not due to a real force acting on you, but rather your body's inertia.

questions.text: During projectile motion, velocity at maximum height is equal to:

The initial velocity (u) can be broken down into its horizontal component (ucos (theta)) and its vertical component (usin (theta)). As the projectile moves upward, the vertical component of velocity decreases due to the influence of gravity until it becomes zero at the maximum height. However, the horizontal component remains unchanged throughout the entire trajectory. Hence, the horizontal component of velocity at the maximum height is equal to ucos (theta), while the magnitude of the total velocity (which includes both horizontal and vertical components) decreases as the projectile reaches its peak.

Digestion is the process by which food is broken down and converted into smaller, simpler molecules that can be absorbed and utilized by the body. It is a crucial part of the digestive system, responsible for extracting nutrients and energy from the food we eat, and supporting various physiological functions.

Photosynthesis is a vital biological process that occurs in green plants, algae, and some bacteria. It is the process by which these organisms convert light energy from the sun into chemical energy in the form of glucose (a type of carbohydrate) while releasing oxygen as a byproduct. Photosynthesis is a key process in the ecosystem, as it is the primary source of energy for most living organisms on Earth.
Respiration and digestion are not anabolic processes. They are catabolic processes which involve break down of larger molecules into smaller molecules. So, this option is not correct.

options.explanation: Drug carriers, also known as drug delivery systems or drug carriers, are specialized formulations or devices designed to transport drugs to specific target sites in the body, enhance drug efficacy, and minimize side effects. The goal of drug delivery systems is to optimize therapeutic effects while minimizing potential harm to healthy tissues. Various types of drug carriers exist, and they can be categorized based on their composition, structure, and mechanism of drug release.

This option is correct because liposomes contain an aqueous core, with water enclosed in the middle by a lipid bilayer. This structure allows them to carry and deliver water-soluble substances, making them important in drug delivery and biological transport systems.

Phospholipids are a class of lipids (fats) that are important structural components of cell membranes in living organisms. They are characterized by having a hydrophilic (water-attracting) head and two hydrophobic (water-repelling) tails. This unique structure gives phospholipids the ability to form lipid bilayers, which are essential components of cell membranes.

questions.text: All children are silly people. Some silly people are rich people. All rich people are big shots. Which of the following conclusions are NECESSARILY TRUE?CONCLUSIONS:I. Some silly people are children.II. Some rich people are children.III. Some silly people are big shots.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I: “Some silly people are children.”This is necessarily true because if all children are silly people, then it logically follows that at least some silly people are children (those children themselves).Conclusion III: “Some silly people are big shots.”This is also necessarily true. We’re told that some silly people are rich people and that all rich people are big shots. Therefore, those same silly people who are rich must also be big shots.Hence, both I and III are logically valid conclusions.

Conclusion II: “Some rich people are children.”This is not necessarily true. Although all children are silly people and some silly people are rich, it doesn’t mean that any of the rich ones are necessarily children — there’s no direct connection between being rich and being a child. The overlap isn’t guaranteed.
While conclusion III is true, conclusion II is not. Hence, this option cannot be correct.
